docs.title: OrgZ
docs.text:
GATTA ORGANIzE — A cross-platform music and radio player built with .NET and Avalonia.

OrgZ helps you manage your local music library and discover internet radio stations from Radio Browser and SHOUTcast, all in one place.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Music Library — Scan and organize your local audio files with full metadata extraction
  • Radio Stations — Browse and stream from over 95,000 internet radio stations
  • Favorites — Star your favorite tracks and stations for quick access
  • Playback Continuity — Navigate freely while your music keeps playing from where you started
  • System Integration — Windows media keys, taskbar controls, and System Media Transport Controls

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Installation
  • First Launch
  • Keyboard Shortcuts
  • Changelog

docs.title: v0.4.20 (Current)
docs.text:
• Abstracted list view system with config-driven columns and context menus
  • Playback context — navigate freely while music continues from where you started
  • Click album art / track info to jump to the currently playing item
  • iTunes-style two-line track display with synchronized marquee scrolling
  • Animated indeterminate seek bar for live radio streams
  • Search clear button
  • View state persistence across restarts (scroll position, selection, search, filters)
  • Radio tags formatted with middot separators
  • Consolas font baseline compensation for Fluent theme controls
  • Darkened status bar background for better contrast

docs.title: v0.4.0
docs.text:
• Initial release
  • Local music library scanning with metadata extraction
  • Radio Browser and SHOUTcast integration
  • Favorites system
  • Windows SMTC and taskbar integration
  • File system watching for library changes

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Access settings via File > Settings or the native menu bar.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Music Library Folder Location — Change or reset the folder OrgZ scans for music
  • Minimize to system tray on close — Keep OrgZ running in the background
  • Remember last played track — Resume playback on next launch

docs.title: Playback
docs.text:
• Streaming Buffer Size — Adjust buffer for radio streams (Small / Medium / Large / Extra Large)
  • Shuffle by — Shuffle by Song or Album
  • Auto-advance to next track — Automatically play the next track when the current one ends

docs.title: Stats
docs.text: View detailed statistics about your library including track counts, file types, total duration, and radio station breakdowns.

docs.title: Advanced
docs.text:
• Database Location — Shows where the SQLite library database is stored
  • Settings File Location — Shows where the JSON settings file is stored
  • Clear Radio Cache — Remove all cached radio station data
  • Reset All Settings — Restore all settings to defaults

docs.title: Favorites
docs.text: Star any music track or radio station to add it to your Favorites list.

docs.title: Adding Favorites
docs.text:
• Right-click a track or station and select Toggle Favorite
  • Click the star icon next to any title in the list

docs.title: Viewing Favorites
docs.text: Click Favorites in the sidebar under Playlists. This view shows all favorited items across both Music and Radio.

docs.title: Playback from Favorites
docs.text: When you start playing from the Favorites view, the playback context follows that list — next/previous and auto-advance work within your favorites, even if you navigate to another view.

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Space Play / Pause Ctrl+Left Previous Track Ctrl+Right Next Track Ctrl+I Show Track Info Ctrl+Q Exit

docs.title: Music Library
docs.text: OrgZ scans your selected folder for audio files and extracts metadata using TagLib.

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text: Format Extensions MPEG Audio .mp3 FLAC .flac AAC .m4a, .aac Ogg Vorbis .ogg Opus .opus WAV .wav WMA .wma APE .ape

docs.title: Columns
docs.text: The Music view shows: Title, Artist, Album, Year, Extension, and Album Art status.

docs.title: File Watching
docs.text: OrgZ monitors your music folder for changes. Added, modified, or deleted files are automatically reflected in the library.

docs.title: Changing Library Folder
docs.text: Go to File > Change Library Folder or open Settings > General to change your music folder location.

docs.title: Playback
docs.text: OrgZ uses LibVLC for audio playback, supporting a wide range of formats and streaming protocols.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
• Play/Pause — Toggle playback
  • Previous/Next — Navigate within the current playback context
  • Volume — Slider with mute and max buttons
  • Seek Bar — Click or drag to seek within a track (disabled for live streams)

docs.title: Playback Context
docs.text:
When you start playing a track, OrgZ captures a snapshot of the current filtered list as your playback context. This means:

  • Auto-advance plays the next track from where you started
  • Previous/Next navigate within that same list
  • Switching views doesn't interrupt playback
  • Starting a track from a different view creates a new context

docs.title: Live Streams
docs.text: Radio streams show a "LIVE" duration and an animated progress indicator instead of a seek bar. Live ICY metadata updates the track info display in real time.

docs.title: Navigate to Playing
docs.text: Click the album art or track info area at the top to jump to the currently playing item in its source view.

docs.title: Per-Track Options
docs.text: OrgZ supports per-track volume adjustment, equalizer presets, and start/stop time markers (configured via the track info dialog).

docs.title: Radio Stations
docs.text: OrgZ integrates with two radio station directories to give you access to thousands of internet radio streams.

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
• Radio Browser — Community-maintained directory of internet radio stations
  • SHOUTcast — One of the oldest and largest streaming directories

docs.title: Getting Started with Radio
docs.text:
1. Click Radio in the sidebar
  2. On first visit, OrgZ fetches the top 250 popular stations from each source
  3. Use the Country and Genre dropdowns at the bottom to filter stations
  4. Double-click a station to start streaming

docs.title: Full Sync
docs.text: Click the sync button in the filter panel to download the complete station database (~95,000 stations). This takes a few minutes but only needs to be done occasionally.

docs.title: Adding Custom Stations
docs.text: Use the + button in the filter panel to add your own stream URL. Format: Station Name|http://stream.url or just the URL.

docs.title: Live Metadata
docs.text: When streaming, OrgZ displays live ICY metadata (artist and title) as it arrives from the station.

docs.title: First Launch
docs.text: When you launch OrgZ for the first time, you'll be prompted to select your music library folder.

docs.title: Selecting Your Music Folder
docs.text:
1. A folder picker dialog will appear
  2. Navigate to the folder containing your music files
  3. Click Select Folder

OrgZ will scan the folder recursively for supported audio files (.mp3, .flac, .m4a, .ogg, .wav, .wma, .ape, .opus) and extract metadata from each file.

docs.title: Interface Overview
docs.text:
The main window is divided into:

  • Top Bar — Playback controls, album art, track info, seek bar, and search
  • Sidebar — Navigate between Music, Radio, and Favorites
  • Content Area — The main list view showing your media
  • Status Bar — Library statistics and sync status

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Browse your Music Library
  • Discover Radio Stations
  • Learn the Keyboard Shortcuts

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
OrgZ is distributed as a self-updating Windows application via Velopack.

  1. Download the latest release from GitHub Releases
  2. Run the installer
  3. OrgZ will automatically check for updates on launch

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Windows 10 or later
  • .NET 10 runtime (bundled with the installer)
